response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, aiming to foster creativity and emotional expression. With 24 residents interested in joining, she divides them into groups of 6 participants each. This ensures that each group receives personalized attention and support, which is crucial for the therapeutic benefits of writing.

Emily plans to allocate 3 writing prompts per group for the session. Since there are 4 groups (24 residents ÷ 6 participants per group = 4 groups), she will need 3 prompts × 4 groups = 12 initial writing prompts. 

In addition to the initial prompts, Emily prepares an extra set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. These prompts will be distributed so that each group can continue writing if they finish the initial set. 

To ensure the workshop runs smoothly, Emily also maintains a library of writing prompts and resources, which she can refer to for inspiration or additional content. She also keeps a library of past sessions to review and adapt for future workshops, ensuring continuity and variety in the writing activities.

In total, Emily needs to have 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, making the total number of writing prompts 22. 

The END.
